subject: Remove non-existent SDNC CSIT job triggers
commit_message:
Remove non-existent SDNC CSIT job triggers

Issue-ID: SDNC-1381
Signed-off-by: Lasse Kaihlavirta <l.kaihlavirt@partner.samsung.com>
Change-Id: I43a395a2d9715e21ac6e368e9f43636051f66a10

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/ci-management-jjb-verify/4970/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/ci-management-jjb-verify/4970

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

See comment below about trigger job - I think this will have the intended effect (i.e. CSIT triggered when new version of docker container is built)

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> See comment below about trigger job - I think this will have the intended effect (i.e. CSIT triggered when new version of docker container is built)

See https://jira.onap.org/browse/CCSDK-2915 (i.e. my understanding is that the SDNC CSIT is currently *not* testing the latest images regardless of how it is triggered)
I agree with the general intention of your proposal - CSIT should be triggered by the docker image build that provides it a new version to test. However, I would postpone adding this trigger now because
1) I am intending to redefine the review chain anyway under https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Moving+CSIT+to+project+repositories and this would be probably changed again as part of that
2) The CSITs are scheduled to run daily even now so you would win only a few hours at best by adding a proper trigger (assuming https://jira.onap.org/browse/CCSDK-2915 was fixed)
3) as you said, scheduled docker image builds would double the daily load (for no obvious additional value) if they triggered CSITs afterwards

> 2) The CSITs are scheduled to run daily even now so you would win only a few hours at best by adding a proper trigger (assuming https://jira.onap.org/browse/CCSDK-2915 was fixed)

Thanks - that makes sense then.
